user_message: Give me the manager view on this Northwind Traders support ticket. The customer escalated this morning.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: account health Discussion - Northwind Traders
Date: 2024-03-25
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Sandra Taylor (Northwind Traders), Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft)

[00:01:30] Sandra Taylor (Northwind Traders): Thanks everyone for joining. Let's dive into account health.

[00:06:00] Sandra Taylor (Northwind Traders): The timeline for budget approval is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:10:00] Sandra Taylor (Northwind Traders): Let me add some context -- we've been working on cost optimization and found that the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk.

[00:12:00] Sandra Taylor (Northwind Traders): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is budget approval.

[00:17:00] Sandra Taylor (Northwind Traders): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about cost optimization. Can we address those?

[00:21:00] Sandra Taylor (Northwind Traders): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is integration complexity.

[00:25:00] Sandra Taylor (Northwind Traders): On the account health front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around technical validation.

handoffs.condition: architecture sketch needed from technical signals

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF44-K trap (No follow-up routing to specialists when transcript surfaced specialist domain); produces complete Architecture summary, blocker inventory (tech/commercial/org), open questions, sequencing recommenda
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF44-K failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF44-K failure mode (No follow-up routing to specialists when transcript surfaced specialist domain)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct account health decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF44-K -- No follow-up routing to specialists when transcript surfaced specialist domain
